refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability of at least the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR contained a conditional phrase 'meet or exceed' which can be simplified to 'achieve at least' to maintain clarity and solution neutrality. The FR focuses solely on the required performance metric (operational availability) without implying any implementation or technology. It is atomic as it specifies a single measurable requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability of at least the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Design the system with redundant critical components to ensure failover capability
Implement continuous system health monitoring and predictive maintenance
Establish clear operational procedures and safety protocols to minimize downtime
Incorporate modular design to allow quick replacement or repair of faulty modules
Use real-time availability tracking and reporting to meet customer-defined targets
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design the system with redundant critical components to ensure failover capability

potential_sources: Redundancy reduces dependency on single components, promoting independence
coupling_check.notes: Redundancy as a DP supports independence by isolating failures and maintaining availability without tightly coupling components.

assumptions_risks: Assumes redundancy can be implemented without excessive cost or complexity
Risk that redundancy may increase system complexity leading to maintenance challenges
Assumes operational policies support and do not conflict with redundancy mechanisms
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and maintenance DPs to complement redundancy and further improve availability assurance.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing system safety, including failover and redundancy of critical components

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts when failover or safety events occur

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives system status and sends safety-related telemetry data

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains many functions beyond failover, leading to high diffusion and low cohesion.
Physical system module; contains some redundancy but failover logic may be spread across other modules.
Focuses on remote control; failover of critical components is peripheral and would cause high diffusion.
Specialized module for safety and failover; high cohesion and low diffusion for the DP.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Safety Services module is the most specific and cohesive boundary to contain the DP of designing redundant critical components for failover, minimizing diffusion and centralizing ownership of safety-related redundancy.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for hardware redundancy implementation.
Interfaces with Telemetry and SafetyAlert must be well-defined to avoid cross-module coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of designing the system with redundant critical components to ensure failover capability directly addresses the FR of achieving operational availability at or above the customer-defined target. Redundancy and failover are standard, effective approaches to meet high availability requirements, aligning well with the acceptance criteria of measured availability ≥ 0.97.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for hardware redundancy implementation.
Interfaces with Telemetry and SafetyAlert must be well-defined to avoid cross-module coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Safety Services module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating it is a cohesive and focused boundary for implementing the DP. Other modules have higher diffusion scores and lower containment, suggesting that centralizing the DP in Safety Services minimizes diffusion risk and improves ownership clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by monitoring system availability metrics and safety alerts generated by the Safety Services module, which manages failover and redundancy.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Safety Services IM is clear and well-justified, with explicit interfaces for safety alerts and telemetry supporting verification and operational monitoring.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and formalize the interfaces between Safety Services and Vehicle modules to manage hardware redundancy dependencies and reduce coupling.
Define strict interface contracts for Telemetry and SafetyAlert to prevent cross-module diffusion and ensure modular integrity.
Document the dependency management strategy between Safety Services and other modules to mitigate diffusion risks and maintain high cohesion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully with clear justification, feasible verification, and well-defined allocation. The DP of designing the system with redundant critical components effectively addresses the FR of achieving operational availability ≥ 0.97. The IM allocation to the Safety Services module shows high containment and low diffusion, minimizing risks. Although some interface clarifications and dependency management are recommended, these are refinements rather than reasons to further decompose the FR. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and cohesive module boundary is preferable to avoid unnecessary complexity. Therefore, no further sub-FR decomposition is needed at this time.

revise.why: Clarify and formalize interfaces between Safety Services and Vehicle modules, define strict interface contracts for Telemetry and SafetyAlert, and document dependency management to reduce diffusion risks and maintain modular integrity.

assumptions: Redundancy and failover are sufficient to meet the availability target.
Safety Services module can effectively encapsulate the DP implementation.
open_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for hardware redundancy implementation.
Interfaces with Telemetry and SafetyAlert require formalization to avoid cross-module coupling.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing system availability ≥ 0.97 under failover conditions.
Documentation of interface contracts and dependency management strategies.
